gives (for Bali): a kind of collar of linen or leather, worn under the kaluṅ by both men and women (dancers). It is app. distinctive of rank or a mark of honour. It seems to extend sidewise; cf ModJ ambapaṅ and suwĕṅ bapaṅ, ``a suwĕṅ, broad on the front''. It is often taluki, or of gold (adorned with gold?) and precious stones. It can be turned back-to-front.
